title: Sansevieria rorida

taxonomy: Sansevieria rorida is a species of flowering plant in the family Asparagaceae, previously classified under the family Agavoideae. However, with the recent revision in plant taxonomy, it has been reassigned to the Asparagaceae family. This species belongs to the genus Sansevieria, a group of plants commonly known as mother-in-law's tongue or snake plant. The Sansevieria rorida is native to tropical regions of Africa and Asia. It is a low-maintenance, adaptable plant species, often grown for its striking, upright leaves.
